cytozip

    • 2. Python API (Quick Overview)

User Guide

  • DNA Methylation Analysis with cytozip
    • 1. Build the reference .cz
    • 3. BAM → .cz
      • BAM → .cz benchmark vs ALLCools
    • 4. ALLC → .cz
    • 5. view and query
      • Warm query: query_numpy vs pytabix across region sizes
      • Correctness check: cytozip query vs pytabix
    • 6. View / query a remote .cz (no download)
    • 7. catcz and merge_cz
    • 8. Build a cell × gene AnnData
  • cytozip Python API
    • view header
    • summary chunks & blocks
    • query
      • Numpy / DataFrame APIs
    • chunk2numpy(dims) — decode a whole chunk
    • chunk2df(dims, reformat=False) — decode as a DataFrame
    • query_numpy(chunk_key, start, end, reference=...)
    • query_numpy_chunk_batch(chunk_key, intervals, reference=...)
    • Decoding many chunks in parallel — chunk2numpy + ThreadPoolExecutor
    • Pack .allc.tsv.gz to .cz without coordinates (using reference)
      • Convert .cz into allc using Python API
      • Remote Reading (from URL)
    • Read remote cz file from webserver
    • Read remote cz file from figshare
  • Calling DMRs with cytozip
    • Pseudobulk vs single-cell input
    • 1. CG DMR — call_dmr
    • 2. CH DMR — call_dmr_ch
    • 3. Batch one-vs-rest — call_dmr_one_vs_rest
      • Stratified one-vs-rest (within each class)
      • CH batch one-vs-rest with shared global-rate pre-scan
    • 4. Merging results — merge_dmr_results
    • 5. CLI equivalents
    • 6. Per-cell methylation over DMRs — cz_to_anndata

Reference

  • Benchmark
    • 1.Storage
  • cz Format — Change History
    • Larger blocks (256 KiB − 1)
    • Wider virtual offsets (44 + 20)
    • Delta-encoded integer columns
    • Tail chunk index (CZIX)
    • Ideas explored but not adopted
  • Developer Guide
    • Architecture overview
    • Module map
      • cz.py — core format
      • cz_accel.pyx — Cython hot paths
      • bam.py — BAM → .cz
      • allc.py — allc table I/O
      • dmr.py — pseudo-reads + bedGraph
      • features.py — AnnData aggregation
      • merge.py — merge .cz files
      • __init__.py — CLI
    • Performance / memory patterns
    • Building the docs locally

cytozip
  • Quick Start Guide
  • Edit on GitHub

Quick Start Guide

github link: czip

1. Installation

pip install czip
# or
pip install git+https://github.com/DingWB/czip.git

[1]:

# Make sure czip is installed: pip install cytozip
!which czip

~/Software/conda/m3c/bin/czip

[2]:

!czip --help

usage: czip [-h] [--version]
            {tocz,catcz,view,summary,extract,allc2cz,build_ref,generate_ssi1,generate_ssi2,merge_cz,merge_cell_type,extractCG,aggregate,combp,annot_dmr}
            ...

cytozip — chunk-based columnar binary format CLI

positional arguments:
  {tocz,catcz,view,summary,extract,allc2cz,build_ref,generate_ssi1,generate_ssi2,merge_cz,merge_cell_type,extractCG,aggregate,combp,annot_dmr}
                        available commands
    tocz                Convert text/stdin to .cz
    catcz               Concatenate multiple .cz files into one
    view                View .cz file contents
    summary             Print chunk summary of a .cz file
    extract             Extract subset of .cz using SSI
    allc2cz             Convert tabix-indexed allc.tsv.gz to .cz
    build_ref           Extract C positions from reference genome
    generate_ssi1       Generate pattern-based SSI (CGN/CHN)
    generate_ssi2       Generate region-based SSI from BED
    merge_cz            Merge per-cell .cz files
    merge_cell_type     Merge by cell type
    extractCG           Extract CG-context records
    aggregate           Aggregate records within regions
    combp               Run comb-p on Fisher results
    annot_dmr           Annotate DMRs

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --version             show program's version number and exit

1. Convert allc/bed/stdin to .cz

[4]:

! czip tocz -h

usage: czip tocz [-h] -O OUTPUT [-I INPUT] [-F FORMATS] [-C COLUMNS]
                 [-D DIMENSIONS] [-u USECOLS] [-d DIM_COLS] [-s SEP]
                 [-c CHUNKSIZE] [--header HEADER] [--skiprows SKIPROWS]
                 [-m MESSAGE] [-l LEVEL]

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -O OUTPUT, --output OUTPUT
                        output .cz file (default: None)
  -I INPUT, --input INPUT
                        input file (stdin if omitted) (default: None)
  -F FORMATS, --formats FORMATS
                        column formats, comma-separated (default: ['B', 'B'])
  -C COLUMNS, --columns COLUMNS
                        column names, comma-separated (default: ['mc', 'cov'])
  -D DIMENSIONS, --dimensions DIMENSIONS
                        dimension names, comma-separated (default: ['chrom'])
  -u USECOLS, --usecols USECOLS
                        column indices to pack, comma-separated (default: [4,
                        5])
  -d DIM_COLS, --dim-cols DIM_COLS
                        dimension column indices (default: [0])
  -s SEP, --sep SEP     separator (default: )
  -c CHUNKSIZE, --chunksize CHUNKSIZE
                        rows per chunk (default: 5000)
  --header HEADER       header row (default: None)
  --skiprows SKIPROWS   rows to skip (default: 0)
  -m MESSAGE, --message MESSAGE
                        message stored in header (default: )
  -l LEVEL, --level LEVEL
                        compression level (default: 6)

[5]:

! czip allc2cz -h

usage: czip allc2cz [-h] -I INPUT -O OUTFILE [-r REFERENCE]
                    [--missing-value MISSING_VALUE] [-F FORMATS] [-C COLUMNS]
                    [-D DIMENSIONS] [-u USECOLS] [--pr PR] [--pa PA] [-s SEP]
                    [--path-to-chrom PATH_TO_CHROM] [-c CHUNKSIZE]

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -I INPUT, --input INPUT
                        input allc.tsv.gz (default: None)
  -O OUTFILE, --outfile OUTFILE
                        output .cz file (default: None)
  -r REFERENCE, --reference REFERENCE
                        reference .cz file (default: None)
  --missing-value MISSING_VALUE
                        missing value fill (default: [0, 0])
  -F FORMATS, --formats FORMATS
                        column formats (default: ['B', 'B'])
  -C COLUMNS, --columns COLUMNS
                        column names (default: ['mc', 'cov'])
  -D DIMENSIONS, --dimensions DIMENSIONS
                        dimension names (default: ['chrom'])
  -u USECOLS, --usecols USECOLS
                        column indices to pack (default: [4, 5])
  --pr PR               position column index in reference (default: 0)
  --pa PA               position column index in input (default: 1)
  -s SEP, --sep SEP     separator (default: )
  --path-to-chrom PATH_TO_CHROM
                        chrom order file (default: None)
  -c CHUNKSIZE, --chunksize CHUNKSIZE
                        rows per chunk (default: 5000)

2. Python API (Quick Overview)

[ ]:

import cytozip as czip

# Read a .cz file using Python API
reader = czip.Reader("test1.cz")
reader.print_header()

[ ]:

# Fetch records for a chunk (dimension)
for record in reader.fetch(("chr1",)):
    print(record)

[ ]:

# Query by coordinate range
for record in reader.query(dimension="chr8", start=3002101, end=3002103, printout=False):
    print(record)
reader.close()

3. Remote Reading (from URL)

czip supports reading .cz files directly from HTTP/HTTPS URLs without downloading the entire file. This uses HTTP Range requests and a chunk index for O(1) lookup.

[ ]:

import cytozip as czip

# Open a remote .cz file via URL
# url = "https://example.com/path/to/file.cz"
# reader = czip.Reader.from_url(url)
# reader.print_header()

# Or simply pass a URL to Reader (auto-detected)
# reader = czip.Reader(url)
# for record in reader.fetch(("chr1",)):
#     print(record)
# reader.close()
print("Remote reading requires a .cz file hosted on an HTTP server supporting Range requests.")
